on behalf of liletric Institutional

lirIRA is a cal,ifornia limited partnership and is registered as

Iaw, DIIRA is required to provide annual disclosure statements to

available for examination by regulatory agencies. These books and

respect to the particular clientts account activity.
tIIRArs business is lirnited to the nanagenent of real estate

government employee benefit plans).

ff consultantrt. However, neither the Forn '72L rlor the Regulations
appear to have been prepared with independent investment nanagers

a number of natters with respect to MIRA.
(1) tlIRArs authority to acguire, manage and sel-I assets on

by SFERS. rs the
definition of rrconsultanttr interpreted so as to distinguish between

employ a }arge
number of professionals, nany of whom will be involved with the

depending upon the particular 
service being

investrnent of funds to acquj-re
properties, the day-to-day management of properti,es or the sale of

purposes?
(3) Porm 721 refers to the disclosure of investrnents held in
the tt j urisdictionrr .

ttin Ia] jurisdictionrt? 
Are there tests such as the issuerrs
principal place of business, percent of revenue or income from a

based, say in Neh, York, rrdoes businessrr in a jurisdiction such as

and obtain confidential treatnent for these filings? Neither these

filing 
of personal financial condition. 
Confidential treatment
woul,d seem appropriate under these circurnstances.
Based on my conversation with Ms. Mccee, I understand that
neither MIRA nor any of its personnel will be required to make a
filing 
under Section 87200 until, 
at the earliest, 
30 days
following execution of the contemplated investment rnanagernent

We appreciate your attention to these inquiries. 
If you need
any further information concerning this request, please feel free
to contact the undersigned at (4L51 378-7LL6.
very truly yours,

sections.question_synthetic: Metric Partners/MIRA seeks FPPC guidance on the applicability of Government Code Section 87200 and Form 721 disclosure requirements to independent investment managers managing public investments, including clarification on consultant definitions, filing party identification, jurisdictional scope of investments, filing timeline commencement, and confidentiality treatment.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from a private party to the FPPC, not an FPPC response. No question/conclusion sections in FPPC format. Contains six numbered questions from the requestor. Document quality affected by fax/scan artifacts and unusual formatting. No evidence this is an FPPC advice letter response.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Metric Partners/MIRA seeks FPPC guidance on the applicability of Government Code Section 87200 and Form 721 disclosure requirements to independent investment managers managing public investments, including clarification on consultant definitions, filing party identification, jurisdictional scope of investments, filing timeline commencement, and confidentiality treatment.

embedding.summary: Metric Partners, on behalf of MIRA (an investment adviser managing real estate portfolios), requests FPPC guidance regarding Form 721 disclosure requirements and Government Code Section 87200 applicability, raising six specific questions about consultant definitions, entity filing responsibilities, investment jurisdiction scope, filing timelines, and potential confidentiality treatment for investment manager filings.
